title: Research Memory Tools
business_process: Before a researcher runs a hypothesis, check the org's own graph first — including abandoned attempts — then the published record, and report what was found in calibrated language.

acceptance_criteria.text: The MCP server starts, registers all six tools, and the host lists them.
The status and edge vocabularies in the tool contracts equal those in the live database DDL.
When the internal check returns a match, the external literature client is never called. Internal-first is asserted, not assumed.
Two phrasings of the same hypothesis return 'matched'; an unrelated hypothesis returns 'no-match'.
A query related to the abandoned finding F-093 returns it, flagged abandoned, with its reason text attached and not ranked below confirmed work for being abandoned.
No tool output ever contains the word 'novel'.
A finding written through write_finding passes the identical validation as one written through the reviewed-write path — the human gate covers both.
A LUSC question about a signature previously tested in LUAD recalls the LUAD finding.
With the network disabled, a previously-cached literature query still returns results.
The rendered wiki page cites the findings it was generated from and is reproducible from the graph alone.
A biologist confirms that a recalled match is a genuine scientific match — it must survive being read aloud on stage.
Fixed prior/posterior fixtures produce stable belief-shift, Bayesian-surprise, entropy, and optional action-divergence values without third-party numerical dependencies.

write_knowledge takes reviewer identity from a separate approval event and rejects missing approval, unlogged or unapproved-model elicitations, unknown source messages, non-verbatim evidence, abandoned items without a reason, conflicting retries, and branching patches without changing the approved row.

recall_knowledge fuses local FTS5/BM25 and dense exact-cosine rankings with reciprocal rank fusion, treats inferred scope as compatible/incompatible/unknown applicability unless strict_scope is explicitly requested, evaluates typed numeric conditions, searches approved aliases, reports the embedding model and score components, resolves historical wording to the active patch, and excludes superseded items by default.

find_experts ranks canonical people from source-linked relevant knowledge and findings using deterministic role weights and a per-session cap, separately returns named active investigators, excludes review-only, investigator-only, non-person, off-disease, and low-coverage matches from demonstrated experts, retains abandoned evidence, exposes all score components and citations, and uses concise evidence-score language without unsupported interpretation.
